title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Changed-Case Lab
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an evidence-first debug and review playbook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Rank competing hypotheses and choose discriminating checks.
Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test prompts and code against misleading context, malformed inputs, and instruction injection.
